title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about heavy rain, damaging winds on Monday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: National Weather Service predicts heavy rain and potential flooding, issuing Flood Watches and High Wind Warnings in Massachusetts.
Meteorologists forecast 1-3 inches of rain with damaging wind gusts up to 65 mph expected Monday night.
Residents in flood-prone areas are advised to prepare as river levels could rise mid-week due to heavy rainfall.

title: Firefighter suffers cardiac arrest during Hyde Park blaze
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A firefighter suffered cardiac arrest during a warehouse fire at 1 Westinghouse Plaza in Hyde Park on Sunday night.
The fire caused approximately $750,000 in damage, with flames spreading across the warehouse roof requiring additional firefighting units.
The cause of the fire is under investigation by the Boston Fire Investigation Unit, with no other injuries reported.

title: Mass. native’s 44-point performance helped Penn reach NCAA Tournament in dramatic fashion
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: T.J. Power scored 44 points to lead Penn to an 88-84 overtime victory against Yale in the Ivy League final.
The win secured Penn an automatic bid to the NCAA Tournament, where they will play Illinois as a 14-seed.
Power, a former Massachusetts Gatorade Player of the Year, transferred to Penn after playing for Duke and Virginia.

title: Hundreds of flights delayed or cancelled at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Logan Airport experienced over 300 flight delays or cancellations Monday due to severe weather on the East Coast.
Massport advises passengers to check flight status as high winds in eastern Massachusetts may reach 70 mph.
Major airlines like Delta, Southwest, and American Airlines are allowing itinerary changes without fees for affected travelers.

title: 11 charged after allegedly staging robberies to claim victim status on immigration applications
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 11 Indian nationals charged with staging robberies in Massachusetts for U Visa applications, according to U.S. Attorney's Office.
Seven defendants arrested in Massachusetts with appearances in Boston federal court; others in Kentucky, Missouri, and Ohio.
Defendant Rambhai Patel sentenced to 20 months in prison; charged with orchestrating and staging armed robberies since March 2023.

title: One injured in South End shooting
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A shooting in the South End on Sunday evening injured one person, who was hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries.
Police responded to the shooting near Washington Street and East Berkeley Street at 7:42 p.m.
No arrests have been made, and the Boston Police Department is actively investigating the incident.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony is emerging as a superstar at World Baseball Classic
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony hit a game-winning home run in Team USA's 2-1 victory over the Dominican Republic in the WBC semifinals.
Anthony has a .318 batting average, two home runs, and seven RBIs over six games in the 2026 World Baseball Classic.
Despite limited MLB experience, the 21-year-old is emerging as a key player for the Red Sox and Team USA.

title: 5 Boston sports moments that just missed out on the Soundbites Bracket
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Rob Gronkowski's 2012 'Yo soy fiesta' quote missed the Soundbites Bracket for Boston sports moments.
Bill Belichick's 'I'm not Mona Lisa Vito' reference in 2015 was omitted due to inclusion of another quote.
Bill Lee's collection of humorous quotes was not included for lacking a single standout soundbite.

title: Oscars 2026: The best jokes from Conan O’Brien’s monologue
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien hosted the Oscars for the second consecutive year at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles on March 15, 2026.
O'Brien's monologue included jokes about Netflix CEO Ted Sarandos and film titles like 'Hamnet' and 'Bugonia'.
Guest Jimmy Kimmel made politically charged jokes, including a dig at CBS and other countries' leaders during his appearance.

title: Fundraiser: Community remembers Danvers mother, nurse killed in her home
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Danvers nurse Janet Swallow, 68, was allegedly killed by 18-year-old Anthony DeMayo in a random attack.
A GoFundMe campaign for Swallow's family has raised nearly $50,000 out of a $65,000 goal.
DeMayo, a high school student, reportedly confessed to the crime and is facing murder charges.
